prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `8fff9e47`.
codex_response:
Implemented `8fff9e47` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_8fff9e47/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_8fff9e47/verifier.py), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_8fff9e47/generator.py). The rule is: split the input into the four row/column parity streams, reverse each stream, and render each one as nested corner bands in its matching output quadrant before concatenating the quadrants. The Opus hint matched the official examples and was used; the Sonnet file had no entry. No new primitive was needed.

The generator stays on-family by sampling dense random `4×n` or `n×4` inputs, constructing the four parity streams explicitly, interleaving them into the input grid, and deriving the output from those same streams rather than calling the verifier. Validation passed end to end: `3/3` official examples, `1000/1000` generated examples, and the previews look consistent with the originals.

Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/8fff9e47/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/8fff9e47/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/8fff9e47/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/8fff9e47/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/8fff9e47/previews/preview_03.png).
